Mathlib Privatization Candidate Dashboard

Last updated: 2026-05-10 19:18 UTC
Summary Methodology Tier 2 — Module bundles Tier 3 — Encapsulation candidates Why some decls are excluded Run history
How to read this dashboard: what the tiers mean and where to look

Candidates are split into three tiers by reference shape. The tabs match the tiers.

TierWhat it groupsCountWhere it shows
Tier 1 One row = one decl. 0 external users, 0 intra-module refs, policy-clean. Applied as a single private edit. 4,269 Rolled up into Tier-2 bundles. Each bundle's decls column lists its tier-1 members.
Tier 2 One row = one module. Modules with ≥ 3 tier-1 decls. One PR per module privatizes its tier-1 set in one commit. Score: blast-cone CP × T30 multiplier (3× if top-30 by leverage) × log(1+edits). 361 Top-25 on Summary; full list under Tier 2 — Module bundles.
Tier 3 One row = one decl. A def whose type is referenced by ≥ 5 decls in the same module, with ≤ 30 external users. Action: move the hub and its dependents into a sub-module that the parent imports privately. 12,382 Tier 3 — Encapsulation candidates tab.

T30: the 30 mathlib modules with the highest leverage (edits × cascade-cost) over the last 1,500 master commits. Acts as a 3× multiplier on the Tier-2 score. Full formula and selection rules under Methodology.

Other tabs. Methodology: how candidates are selected, bundled, and scored. Why some decls are excluded: per-decl reasoning for non-candidates (blocked attribute, forbidden name pattern, registered in an extension's by-name registry, etc.). Run history: per-run tier counts and transitions (new / merged / dropped).

Modules with mechanically-safe privatization available. Each row groups all tier-1 candidates in that module. Score combines blast-cone CP × top-30 multiplier × log(1+edits).

14.8 3 T30 6
Mathlib.Order.Hom.Basic
  • «term_→o_» candidate
  • «term_↪o_» candidate
  • «term_≃o_» candidate
 ready to PR

Methodology

The dashboard lists mathlib4 declarations whose reference shape and attribute profile permit a private annotation. A private decl's body lives in .olean.private and is excluded from the public .olean. Body-only edits to private decls leave downstream .olean.hash unchanged. Cache-cut empirics: REPORT.md. Theoretical aggregate ceiling: ~92% rebuild-CP savings once api-hash caching ships.

1. Selecting candidate declarations

scripts/decl_census/DeclCensus.lean walks every declaration in mathlib's elaborator state. Per decl, it emits a JSONL row with fq_name, defining module, kind, attributes, name pattern, has-docstring, and reference counts from Expr.foldConsts over info.type and info.value?.

FieldWhat it counts
n_external_users References from other modules.
n_intra_module_refs References from the same module.
n_signature_refs Same-module references that occur in the type signature, not the body.
name_pattern Classifier flag: normal, underscore_prefix, simps_projection, auto_derived, etc.
meta_consumers Extension registries (@[simps], @[simp], …) that contain this decl by name.

2. Filtering

Each decl passes through policy.toml's hard_blocks before becoming a candidate. policy.toml is consumed by rerank, apply, and the dashboard.

LayerBlocksReason
forbidden_attrs @[reducible], @[implicit_reducible], @[deprecated], @[inline] Semantic attributes whose behavior interacts with visibility.
build_rejected_attrs @[simp], @[norm_cast], @[ext], @[macro], @[fun_prop], @[positivity], @[norm_num], @[simps], @[instance], … Extensions that look up the decl by name at attribute elaboration. Lean rejects private on a decl carrying any of these.
forbidden_name_patterns simps_projection (decls under Foo.Simps.*). Generated by @[simps] on the parent struct. Consumed by name without carrying an attribute themselves.
forbidden_module_prefixes Mathlib.Tactic., Mathlib.Meta., Mathlib.Lean. Elaborator-helper directories. Candidates from here are rejected at apply (forbidden-attr filter) or reverted at build. Pre-filtering drops ~14% of candidate churn.

Soft block: theorems and lemmas with a docstring and name_pattern=normal are treated as public API regardless of external-user count.

3. Tiering

TierMembershipUnit of action
Tier 1 n_external_users == 0, n_intra_module_refs == 0, intent-safe, policy-clean. One private edit per decl.
Tier 2 Modules with ≥ 3 Tier-1 decls (grouped by defining_module). One PR per module.
Tier 3 kind ∈ {def, abbrev}, n_signature_refs ≥ 5, n_external_users ≤ 30. Move the hub and its in-signature dependents into a sub-namespace that the parent imports privately. Worked example: mathlib4 #38702.

4. Scoring

Per-module weight, used to rank tier-2 bundles:

score(m) = (1 − exp(−bcp / 100))    × (3 if T30 else 1)    × (1 + log(1+edits) / 3)
           ─────────────────────      ─────────────────      ──────────────────────
           cascade-cost saturation    leverage multiplier    edit-frequency factor
TermDefinitionSource
bcp Blast-cone critical-path seconds: the CP of the induced subgraph over all transitive importers of m. Per-module property of the build graph; independent of commit content. pipeline/p07_churn_blast.py
T30 1 if m is in the 30 highest-leverage modules in the 1,500-commit window, else 0. Leverage = edits × bcp. pipeline/out/churn_blast.json:top_leverage
edits Commits in the 1,500-commit window that touched m's source. git log walk over master.

Per-tier scoring:

  • Tier 1: each decl inherits its module's score(m).
  • Tier 2: bundle score is score(m) weighted by n_decls.
  • Tier 3: bcp × n_signature_refs.

5. Top-30 leverage list (T30)

Computed once per pipeline run from a walk of 1,500 commits of mathlib master, attributing each commit's blast-cone CP to the modules it touched, sorted by aggregate leverage. Current top-5:

#ModuleEdits (1500-commit window)blast-CP-s per editLeverage
1Mathlib.SetTheory.Cardinal.Cofinality25376.89,420
2Mathlib.SetTheory.Ordinal.Basic20399.77,994
3Mathlib.Tactic.Translate.Core10713.87,138
4Mathlib.Data.List.Basic9669.76,027
5Mathlib.Tactic.Translate.ToDual8707.85,663

Membership shifts as the import graph churns. The dashboard re-reads the list on every regen.

6. Verification

Each retained privatization is verified by lake build. Two pipelines:

  • Bulk (pr1/bulk_apply.py + bulk_iterate.sh): apply every tier-1 candidate in one commit, then loop build → revert breaking decls → rebuild until convergence. Output: one diff, full-mathlib verified.
  • Per-bundle (pr1/iterate_bundle.sh): apply per module with a 4-importer build cap. Output: a series of small commits.

Both pipelines end with a full-mathlib build. Apply/revert unit tests: tests/. Refer to LANDSCAPE.md §6.5.

All tier-2 module bundles (361)

Sortable and searchable. Click a row's module to see candidate decls and current status.

Score N decls Top-30 Edits Module / Decls Action

Tier-3 sub-module encapsulation candidates (12382)

Tier-3 selection: heuristic and suggested actions

Each row is one declaration. Acting on the row means moving the declaration plus the same-module declarations that mention it in their type signature into a sub-namespace that the parent imports privately.

Hub definition

A declaration D in module M is a hub when at least 5 of M's other declarations mention D in their type signature, not just their proof body. Type-signature references cannot be removed by editing only the consumer; body references can.

Selection

FilterThresholdReason
kind def or abbrev Theorem/lemma decls are facts; the encapsulation pattern applies to constructive definitions.
n_signature_refs ≥ 5 Smaller clusters do not warrant a sub-module split.
n_external_users ≤ 30 Each external consumer must add a public import of the new sub-module.
policy.toml hard blocks Same gates as Tier 1 and Tier 2 (forbidden attributes, name patterns, module prefixes).

Suggested actions

Selected by n_external_users:

n_external_usersActionMechanics
0 Privatize wholesale (cluster + hub) Mark the hub and every in-signature dependent private. One file edited, no sub-module split.
1 – 10 Sub-module encapsulation refactor Split Mathlib/Foo.lean into Mathlib/Foo.lean + Mathlib/Foo/Internal.lean. The parent private imports the new sub-module. Each external consumer adds a public import. Worked example: mathlib4 #38702.
11 – 30 Discuss first Consumer-side work scales linearly with external users. The refactor cost may exceed the cache-cut benefit, or the hub may be public API.

Each row below is one hub. The Co-located decls cell expands to the same-module declarations that would move with it. Auto-derived names (proof obligations, equation lemmas, simp markers) are folded under a nested toggle.

Each row is one hub declaration. The Co-located decls column lists same-module decls that reference the hub in their type signature. These would move into a sub-module alongside the hub if extracted. Auto-derived names (proof obligations, equation lemmas) are folded under a nested toggle.

Score Sig refs Ext users Doc Hub Module Co-located decls Status Suggested action
